title: About
text:
Eigenholm is a personal technical site and research notebook focused on machine learning, deep learning, mathematics, and scientific computing.
The writing here covers paper analyses, implementation notes, reproductions of published results, and documentation of technical projects. The goal is to think carefully, build from first principles, and share what is learned along the way.

A Python example

import numpy as np 
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

x = np.linspace(-3, 3, 200) 
y = x**2

plt.plot(x, y) 
plt.xlabel("x") 
plt.ylabel("x²") 
plt.title("Quadratic function") 
plt.show()

title: Article Title Here
text:
Mathematical content
Inline math uses single dollars: the loss function \mathcal{L}(\theta) measures model error.
Display equations use double dollars:
\nabla_\theta \mathcal{L}(\theta) = -\frac{1}{N} \sum_{i=1}^{N} \nabla_\theta \log p_\theta(x_i) {#eq-gradient}
Reference equations with @eq-label: see ?@eq-gradient above.
A matrix example:
A = \begin{bmatrix}
a_{11} & a_{12} \\
a_{21} & a_{22}
\end{bmatrix}, \quad
\lambda \in \sigma(A)

title: Article Title Here
text:
Code

Static code block (not executed)
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(dim, dim),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Linear(dim, dim),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return x + self.net(x)


Executable code block
Uncomment jupyter: python3 in the YAML header to enable execution.
```{python}
#| label: fig-example
#| fig-cap: "Example plot generated from code."

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

x = np.linspace(-2 * np.pi, 2 * np.pi, 300)
y = np.sin(x) * np.exp(-0.1 * x**2)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
ax.plot(x, y, color="#2a6e6e", linewidth=1.5)
ax.set_xlabel("x")
ax.set_ylabel("f(x)")
ax.set_title("Damped sine wave")
ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```


Inline code
Use backticks for inline code: the Adam optimizer with lr=3e-4.

Definitions and terminology

Term

Eigenvalue

A scalar \lambda such that Av = \lambda v for some nonzero vector v.

title: Article Title Here
text: References

title: Colophon
text: Eigenholm is an independent technical website and research notebook maintained by Diego Filippo Marino.

title: Colophon
text:
Purpose
This site publishes research notes, paper analyses, implementation details, and technical experiments. The goal is to think carefully about problems in machine learning, mathematics, and scientific computing — and to document what is learned along the way.
Articles here are written for a technical audience. They aim to be precise, reproducible, and useful.

title: Colophon
text:
Tools
This site is built with Quarto, an open-source scientific publishing system. Mathematics is rendered with KaTeX. Code is executed in Python via Jupyter kernels.
The source is version-controlled with Git and hosted on GitHub. Deployment is automated through GitHub Actions to GitHub Pages.

title: Colophon
text:
Typography
Headings use Inter, a typeface designed for computer screens. Body text uses the browser’s default serif stack. Code is set in the system monospace font.

title: Colophon
text:
Design
The visual identity is intentionally restrained: generous whitespace, a muted teal accent, uppercase headings with wide tracking, and minimal decoration. The coordinate-dot motif references the mathematical meaning of eigen — characteristic values, fixed points, structure.

title: Colophon
text:
Citing articles
Each article on this site includes automatically generated citation metadata. Use the citation appendix at the bottom of any article to reference it in your own work.

title: Colophon
text:
Contact

For questions, corrections, or collaboration, open an issue on the GitHub repository.

title: Notes
text:
Short-form research notes, reading logs, and work-in-progress observations.
